feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to change a numeric value to a percentage value in a numeric point chart in SAC?
entry.content:
I have a numeric point chart which shows some value & this value is coming from a filter / input_control in SAC.

I want to use a button to switch between a numeric value & a percentage value in the chart. Is this possible to achieve? Kindly share solutions, if possible!

entry.title: Bug Report – Dynamic Text Boxes Not Updating on Subpages When Navigating Directly
entry.content:
We have a story with 28 pages in total—4 main pages and 24 subpages. Our design uses four date/year input controls located on the main pages to update dynamic text boxes across all pages. Each main-page input control is responsible for updating dynamic text boxes on approximately 6–7 subpages.

Issue:
When users bypass the main pages and jump directly to subpages, the dynamic text boxes fail to update. It appears that the necessary data and input control configurations are not loaded because the user did not visit the main page where the controls reside. As a result, the date data is not reflected on the subpages.

Impact:

  • User Experience: End users see outdated or missing date information on subpages.
  • Performance: Adding duplicate input controls to each subpage is not a viable solution as it degrades overall performance.

Request:
Could you please advise on a solution or workaround that ensures dynamic text boxes on subpages load correctly even when users navigate directly to them? We are looking for an approach that does not require placing input controls on every page.

Thank you for your assistance in resolving this issue.

#SAC #SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: SAC - Block/overwrite old bookmarks
entry.content:
Hi community,

in one of our Input Templates, we have made some changes since the last planning cycle. We have added a source column, which leads to data beeing written on, lets say, "Manual Adjustment". In the old version, we didnt had that column, hence data was writte to source "Unassigned".

If users use their old bookmarks, they still see the old version of the input template and data is written on unassigned, which is a problem.

I could copy the Input Template and dont include the bookmarks, but the users wouldnt be amused about creating new bookmarks.

Is there a solution? For example keeping the selected Filters in the bookmark, but apply them to the new input template?

Do you need further explanation?

Kind regards,

Eric

entry.title: Creating a geomap widget in SAC from a live data model from PaPM
entry.content:
Hello folks,

I'm triying to create a Geomap (I'm skipping the steps to create the story's widget) in SAC while using a live data model to PaPM. But there's little or no information/documentation available to follow the steps in order to create the "geodimension" to make the geomap widget. 

However, there is this link (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/f7f432923cd9495bb5c98d5cdd667663.html)  where explains that IT'S possible to create a "geodimension" with a Hana Calculation View using a live connetion. But the problem lies that the client only has PaPM as a source data, no Hana Studio is available. 

Is there any documentation or knows the proper steps to create the "geodimension" in order to add the geomap widget. 

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAC Advance Formula Error while copying data from one model to other model
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to copy data from one model A to another planning model B with MODEL and LINK command. With this I also want to populate dimensions of Model B using properties of Model A dimension. I am using following script but it gives me error of Scope redirection.

//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
//Set Configurations Definitions for the Advanced Formulas.
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONFIG.TIME_HIERARCHY = FISCALYEAR
CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
//The data region against which the formulas will be executed.
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = ("MDP")
MEMBERSET [d/FISCAL_MONTH] = BASEMEMBER([d/FISCAL_MONTH].[h/FYP], %FISCAL_YEAR%)
MEMBERSET [d/DATA_SOURCE] = ("UPLOAD_PTA_SLS")
//MEMBERSET [d/ACCOUNT] = "CGRETSL"
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
//Calculation and Data Writing
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Link PTA Actuals Model to transfer data
MODEL [SaFARI - PTA Actuals]
ENDMODEL
 
// Copy Data to Planning model and assign properties of Base Code to Estimate Code, Factory and Product to respective dimension
DATA(
    [d/Measures] = "MDP", 
[d/ESTIMATE_CODE] = [SaFARI - PTA Actuals].[d/BASECODE].[p/EST_CODE],
[d/FACTORY] = [SaFARI - PTA Actuals].[d/BASECODE].[p/FACTORY],
    [d/SaFARI_PRODUCT] = [SaFARI - PTA Actuals].[d/BASECODE].[p/PRODUCT]
 
) = LINK(
    [SaFARI - PTA Actuals],
    [d/Version] = "public.Actual",
    [d/Measures] = "MDP"
)
 
------
Below are the error messages I get with this code.
- There is violation of scope redirection in the calculation expressions.
- The dimension [SaFARI - PTA Actuals].BASECODE] is not allowed to be used with linked model because it is shared dimension.
 
Appreciate any help on this. Thanks!

entry.title: best design for using SAP SAC data into a SAP Fiori - CDS flavour
entry.content:
Hello

User case: We need to solve a consumption scenario based on SAP Sac calculated data into SAP S/4 backend

Users will need to consult a field (CeCo) that will be available on SAP SAC models. The initial proposed design is based on the construction of a CDS-OData flavour that connect to SAC, pull the data and show to the backend users. Scope +1500 SAP backend users

Can someone share her/his opinion about this scenario, the best approach, the alternatives and their knowledge?

Can we think in some kind of SDA federation of the data, can we persist the data in an intermediate layer (i,e BW) o whatever?

I will appreciate your help in advanced

Kind regards

entry.title: Is it possible to read the data from analytical view and write it back to SAC planning model
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to read the data from analytical view from SAP datasphere and write it back to SAC planning model ?

I know we can do this by importing the data from SAP Datasphere to SAC via Odata connection but is it possible via scripting?

Anyone who has faced this issue or knows the workaround in this case. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: SAC - Advanced Formula Data Action - Calculate Prior Previous Year Ending Balances.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We are currently facing a challenge in SAP Analytics Cloud concerning data action using advanced formulas, specifically regarding the calculation of previous years' figures within the Account and Date dimensions. We are utilizing an Account-Based Model perform forecasts on our Balance Sheet Model.

 

Requirement:

Our objective is to calculate the Prior Year Average Balance for future periods based on actual data. The formula we would like to implement is as follows:

=(PY-1 End Bal + PY End Bal)/2

 Example:

For example, when we are on **January 2025**, our calculation should reference the following values:

 

If I am in any month in any month of current year, it has to pick last year Dec Ending values of Accounts Payable data from Account Dimension – Prior Previous Year Dec Data for Account Payable data from Account Dimension.

 

Chart:

 

2023.Dec

2024.Jan

2024.Feb

2024.Mar

2024.Apr

2024.May

2024.Jun

2024.Jul

2024.Aug

2024.Sep

2024.Oct

2024.Nov

2024.Dec

2025.Jan

10

12

13

15

15

15

20

20

25

25

30

35

40

50 (Expected)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

- **2024 Dec** (Current Year End Balance)

- **2023 Dec** (Prior Year End Balance)

 

Thus, the expected result for **2025 Jan** would be:

On Jan 2025 When I run data action it should calculate : =(PY-1 End Bal + PY End Bal)

2024.Dec + 2023

40               +    10  Expected Results = 50 on 2025.Jan

 

 

Current Approach:

At present, I am utilizing the `PREYEARLAST` function to retrieve last year's ending data. However, I am encountering difficulties in formulating a straightforward syntax to fetch the data from the Prior Previous Year.

 

To address this, I have implemented the following workaround, although I have concerns regarding its efficiency:

 

IF [d/Date].[p/MONTHDESC] = "Jan" THEN

  DATA([d/Account]="Stat_PYAvgBal_AP") =   (RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Stat_AP", [d/Date]=PREYEARLAST([d/Date])) +

    RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Stat_AP", [d/Date]=PREVIOUS(13, "MONTH", [d/Date]))) / 2

```

 

This approach retrieves data for all 12 months of the current year while going back 24 months, which may not be optimal.

 

Request for Assistance:

I would greatly appreciate any insights or recommendations on achieving this calculation in a more efficient manner. Your expertise in this area would be invaluable.

 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

 

Regards,

Ramesh

entry.title: Data Locking Ownership logs
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Recently we encountered in our planning model, DLO toggle was on OFF status, however about a month or so we enabled it.

I tried digging up activity logs but nothing points specific to DLO change, I checked imported transports if they had nay impact from source system and it all looks in place except the target system.

your directions are much appreciated, thanks

entry.title: Add line in table - SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi! 

Is there any way to create a button that, when clicked, creates a line in the table to be filled, like add member?

entry.title: Needed ! SAC Story Table "unhide" a hidden row
entry.content:
Hi,

Problem is:
-using SAC with Live Model and a table.
-the table offers the feature "Hide row" to hide away rows defined by the developer ( not filtering !!!).

How to unhide hidden values ? 
How or where to get (accidently) hidden values back without setting up the table from new ?

ThanXs 
Martin

entry.title: SAC User Attribute Mapping
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I understand there are multiple ways to create users in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC):

1. Dynamic User Creation and

2. Provisioning via IPS.

Which method is generally recommended, and under what cases?

Similarly, for assigning user attributes, there are two options:

1. SAML Attribute Mapping and

2. IPS Transformation.

"targetPath": "$['urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:group-roles']['roles'][1]['value']",
"condition": "$.displayName == 'group'",
"constant": "PROFILE:sap.epm:Admin",

Which method is recommended, and when should each method be used?

 

Many thanks

entry.title: SAC SAML Mapping - Groups Attribute Not Visible in SAC
entry.content:
Hello all,

I am configuring SAML SSO in SAC and trying to map the Groups attribute under SAML Attribute Mapping. However, I cannot see the "Groups" attribute in SAC.

The SAML Tracer shows that the attribute (Group) is being send in the SAML assertion.

<Attribute Name="Groups">

<AttributeValue>d50b9d19-460b-484c-87f4-0f57817f064c</AttributeValue>

Can Azure Groups be assigned this way?

Many Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: Can we plan currency rates in sap analytics planning
entry.content:
Hi, 

Can anyone please help to explain if we can plan currency rates for future time periods in SAC like BPC?

If so, can you please explain the process for the same.

Regards,

Lakshmi

@N1kh1l

entry.title: Issue data blending in Story using table
entry.content:
I have two different Models in SAC and I want to combine this two model in a story and create a table visualization. 

Both Models has been linked by a common dimension member avaialbe in both models. Everything is working properly when using  a Chart in the story. But NOT more for Table. 

Could some one support me?

Best regards,

Linking 2 Models in Story by using Table is not working

 

Linking 2 Models in Story by using Chart is working

entry.title: Re: Issue data blending in Story using table
entry.content:
Hi ,

I think you are using optimized story experience.  Please note the "Blending" option for chart is available currently, however 'Blending' for tables - are yet to be released. its in the roadmap. 

Please note the blending options for classic story is available. if your scenario demands that option is .

Please refer unsupported features as of today : 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/2d00f5ff0c9245a99a9df96cb83236a9.html

 

Workaround : 

1. as a work around use the classic story. 

2. perform the joining in the back end and use it the joined model in the front end.

Thanks with regards,

Giri

SAP

entry.title: Display a new key figure as a weighted sum based on a dimension
entry.content:
Dear community: 

I have recently created a new key figure, which is a multiplication of two other key figures. I want to be able to display the weighted summary of it, based on a dimension called "ages". 

 

right now, the total is display without taking in consideration the dimension i have added, and i do not know how to do to weight the total based on this dimension. 

Does somebody know how to do it? 

 

thanks!

entry.title: Stopping blank reports whilst bursting
entry.content:
Hi, we have a story which shows our sellers quotes for the day, we have this set up using data access controls and a publication in burst mode. Is there anyway to only send a report when there is data for them? Currently everyone on the seller team receives a report even if blank.
Any advice appreciated, thank you.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Threshold Visualization Issue
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

currently I'am observing following issue with visualizing thresholds. 

I set up the threshold like this:

1. 

2. 

3. 

Now. When I save the story and open it in View mode the throsholds aren't shown. 

View Mode:

I believe the issue might be related to the threshold setting, as the Measure under Step 2 is populated by an element from the "FIBUPe Time Slices" dimension. However, this element is not actually a measure.

What I don't understand is—why does it work in edit mode?

Can anyone explain?

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: ID and Description in SAP Datasphere using Business Builder
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to build a report in the SAP Analytics Cloud based on a data model from SAP Datasphere, which among other things has the dimension "Product". The product should be able to be displayed as an ID and / or as a Description.
Therefore, I have created all the necessary tables, text tables and views in the Data Builder in SAP Datasphere and linked them together. If I now create an Analytic Model in the Data Builder (assigning the semantic relationships of attributes and texts) and use this in SAP Analytics Cloud, I can choose between ID and Description there.
Instead of the Data Builder, I would like to create a Consumption Model with the Business Builder and use it in SAP Analytics Cloud, with which the ID-Description-assignment unfortunately does not work; only the ID is displayed.
I have made the following configurations for this:

  • Attribut "ARTNR" (describes the product) within Fact

     

  • Attribute "ARTNR" cannot be selected in Cosumption Model: There are two attributes "ARTNR.ID" and "ARTNR.TEXT" now ("ARTNR.TEXT" can't be selected)
  • Attribute "ARTNRID" within Cosumption Model

     

  • Preview of "ARTNRID" only shows the IDs

I would like to ask for support.

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,

Marcel
